title: Icy Intense Burgundy Celery Gratin
description: A pungent, chewy French gratin that pairs crisp celery with a rich Burgundy cream sauce, finished with a chilling ice-bath shock for a romantic dining experience.
instructions: In a large pot, simmer the sliced celery in salted water for 5 minutes, then drain and shock it in an ice bath of 500 ml water for 2 minutes.
Pat the celery dry with a towel and place it in a greased 1.5 L baking dish.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat and add the minced garlic, cooking until fragrant for 1 minute.
Pour in the white wine and reduce by half, then stir in the heavy cream and allow the sauce to thicken for 3 minutes.
Season the sauce with thyme, salt, and pepper, then pour it evenly over the chilled celery.
Sprinkle the grated Gruyère cheese over the top and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Remove the gratin, let it rest for 5 minutes, then carefully transfer the dish into a large bowl of ice water to cool rapidly.
Serve the gratin straight from the ice bath for a dramatic, chilled presentation.
Garnish with an extra thyme sprig before serving.
